skill.name: firebase-security-rules-auditor
skill.description: A skill to evaluate how secure Firestore security rules are. Use this when Firestore security rules are updated to ensure that the generated rules are extremely secure and robust.
skill.summary: Systematically audit Firebase Firestore security rules for vulnerabilities, privilege escalation, and misconfigurations using a proven scoring framework.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: All 8 static findings are false positives. The external_commands detection flagged Markdown inline code backticks (hasOnly(), diff()) as shell execution, which is incorrect. The weak cryptographic algorithm detection on lines 3 and 41 has no basis - line 3 is YAML frontmatter and line 41 is a JSON output example. The system reconnaissance findings refer to legitimate security auditing methodology for Firebase rules analysis. No prompt injection attempts detected. The skill is a well-structured security auditing tool that analyzes Firestore security rules without executing commands or exfiltrating data.

content.user_title: Audit Firebase Security Rules for Vulnerabilities
content.value_statement: Firebase security rules are complex and easy to misconfigure. This skill systematically evaluates Firestore security rules against proven audit criteria to find vulnerabilities before attackers do.
actual_capabilities: Evaluates Firestore security rules against a 6-point mandatory audit checklist
Detects privilege escalation vulnerabilities in create versus update rule mismatches
Identifies missing ownership checks and authorization gaps in access control logic
Flags resource exhaustion risks from missing size limits and type constraints
Scores rule security on a 1 to 5 scale with detailed findings and recommendations
Validates admin bootstrapping logic for proper email verification checks
limitations: Only evaluates Firestore security rules, not Realtime Database or Cloud Storage rules
Cannot test rules against live data or simulate real user traffic patterns
Requires the user to provide the complete security rules for accurate analysis
Does not evaluate backend server-side security or authentication configuration

use_cases.title: Pre-Deployment Security Review
use_cases.description: A developer reviews updated Firestore security rules before deploying to production to catch vulnerabilities early in the release cycle.

use_cases.title: Incident Response Investigation
use_cases.description: A security engineer investigates a potential data breach by auditing the current Firestore security rules for weaknesses and misconfigurations.

use_cases.title: Compliance and Best Practices Audit
use_cases.description: A team lead performs a quarterly audit of all Firestore security rules to ensure compliance with internal security standards and industry best practices.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Rule Review
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wants to check simple security rules for obvious issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Firestore security rules and check for any obvious security issues: [paste your rules here]

prompt_templates.title: Full Audit with Scoring
prompt_templates.scenario: Auditor needs a comprehensive evaluation with severity scores
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a complete security audit of these Firestore security rules using the mandatory audit checklist. Score each area on a 1 to 5 scale and provide specific findings with recommendations. Rules: [paste your rules here]

prompt_templates.title: Create and Update Rule Comparison
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer needs to check for create and update bypass vulnerabilities
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the create and update rules in these Firestore security rules. Identify any scenarios where a user could create a valid document and then update it into an invalid or malicious state. Rules: [paste your rules here]

prompt_templates.title: Ownership and Access Control Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Security reviewer wants to verify authorization logic for multi-user documents
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the ownership and access control logic in these Firestore security rules. Identify any operations that lack proper ownership checks or allow unauthorized access to documents. Rules: [paste your rules here]

output_examples.input: Review these Firestore rules for a social media app with user profiles and posts.
output_examples.output: The audit found a critical vulnerability in the user profile rules. The create rule checks ownership but the update rule does not verify the requesting user owns the document. This allows any authenticated user to modify any user profile. Overall score: 2 out of 5.

output_examples.input: Audit collaboration app security rules for team documents.
output_examples.output: The rules are generally secure with proper ownership checks on user documents. However, the team document rules use hasOnly without an ownership check, allowing any authenticated user to modify team data. Adding resource.data.ownerId equals request.auth.uid would fix this. Score: 3 out of 5.

best_practices: Always verify ownership checks are present for every write operation, not just create operations.
Use type checking functions like is string and is int on all document fields to prevent data corruption.
Compare create and update rules side by side to detect privilege escalation through update bypasses.
anti_patterns: Applying hasOnly or diff restrictions without corresponding ownership verification, which limits field changes but does not prevent unauthorized users from making them.
Trusting user-provided data for sensitive fields like role or isAdmin without verifying against a secure authority source.
Using the same rules for create and update without verifying the update operation cannot bypass create-time restrictions.

faq.question: What types of Firebase rules can this skill evaluate?
faq.answer: This skill is designed to evaluate Firestore security rules only. It does not evaluate Realtime Database or Cloud Storage security rules.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my Firebase security rules?
faq.answer: No, this skill only analyzes and evaluates rules you provide. It does not make any changes to your Firebase configuration or deploy new rules.

faq.question: How are security scores calculated?
faq.answer: Scores range from 1, critical vulnerabilities, to 5, fully secure. The evaluation checks for update bypasses, authority sources, business logic, storage limits, type safety, and access control.

faq.question: Can this skill replace a professional security audit?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides automated analysis but cannot replace a comprehensive professional security audit or penetration test performed by a security expert.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide for the audit?
faq.answer: You need to provide your complete Firestore security rules. For best results, also describe your app data model and intended access patterns.

faq.question: Does the skill check for common attack patterns?
faq.answer: Yes, it checks for privilege escalation, data corruption, unauthorized access, resource exhaustion, and type safety violations in your security rules.
